_peak_flops_tf_convention: The values below are the EFFECTIVE per-precision compute baseline used by AC's roofline model, NOT the datasheet peak. They are deliberately set to ~50% of NVIDIA's marketed dense-Tensor-Core peak so that, after the `efficiency_multipliers` block and the per-family efficiency table in throughput_model._DEFAULT_EFFICIENCY_TABLE compose, end-to-end TPS / TBT predictions match published NeMo, Megatron-LM, and vLLM traces on H100. See `notes.peak_flops_source` for datasheet numbers. Override via ac-auto-calibrate when lab traces are available.
_datasheet_peak_flops_tf_note: Wave 20 (feedback #2): vendor datasheet dense Tensor-Core peaks, for implied-MFU reporting ONLY. The roofline model must keep using peak_flops_tf. On NVIDIA parts peak_flops_tf is ~half of these (see _peak_flops_tf_convention); on TPU/Trainium the two coincide.

calibration._prefill_multiplier_note: v1-fix demo-audit-2 (Jun 2026): prefill bumped 1.4 -> 2.5 to match published FA-3 throughput on H100. The previous 1.4 gave net 0.84 of half-peak ≈ 21% of true FP8 peak, which was 3-4x too pessimistic vs FlashAttention-3 traces (Llama-2-7B @ 8k batch=1 ~50-80ms; the previous calibration reported ~290ms). At 2.5 the net achievable is 0.6 * 2.5 = 1.5x half-peak ≈ 75% of true BF16 peak ≈ 38% of true FP8 peak when FFN is FP8 — matches Dao 2024 FA-3 prefill benchmark.

notes.chip_reuse: H800 SXM is the export-restricted sibling of the H100 SXM: identical GH100 silicon, identical per-GPU compute / HBM / SRAM / calibration parameters. Every field except `accelerator_name` and `interconnect.intra_node_bw_gb_s` is copied verbatim from h100_sxm.json. NVIDIA H800 GPU datasheet (mirrored copy, https://www.scribd.com/document/777167019/NVIDIA-H800-GPU-Datasheet , accessed 2026-07-17, tier T2): H800 SXM rows match H100 SXM — 80 GB HBM3, 3.35 TB/s, BF16 1979 TFLOPS with sparsity (989 dense), FP8 3958 with sparsity (1979 dense), up to 700W.
notes.nvlink_bandwidth_source: H800 SXM NVLink 4 aggregate GPU-to-GPU bandwidth is 400 GB/s, reduced from 900 GB/s on H100 SXM (US export-control compliance SKU). NVIDIA H800 GPU datasheet interconnect row (mirror above, tier T2); corroborated by TrendForce/Reuters reporting on the H800 SKU and by DeepSeek-V3's training-hardware disclosure (H800 nodes, NVLink 400 GB/s) — https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.19437 (accessed 2026-07-17, tier T2/T3).
notes.peak_flops_source: NVIDIA H100/H800 SXM5 datasheet (dense, no 2:4 sparsity). BF16 Tensor Core = 989 TFLOPS; TF32 Tensor Core = 494.7 TFLOPS; FP8 Tensor Core = 1979 TFLOPS. The `peak_flops_tf` field above is intentionally ~half of these (see `_peak_flops_tf_convention`). https://nvidia.com/en-us/data-center/h100/ (accessed 2026-07-17, tier T2).
notes.fused_efficiency_source: FlashAttention-2/3 benchmarks on H100, typical 0.75-0.85 range, using 0.80 midpoint
notes.hbm_bw_source: NVIDIA H800 SXM datasheet, 3.35 TB/s HBM3 (same as H100 SXM).
notes.efficiency_multipliers_source: Inherited from h100_sxm.json: calibrated to NeMo / Megatron-LM Llama-3-70B BF16 published throughput (~1500-1800 tok/s/GPU at TP=8, batch>=64) and to published vLLM decode TBT for 7B/70B chat. Compute-side efficiency transfers to H800 unchanged (same silicon); the NVLink reduction is expressed purely through interconnect.intra_node_bw_gb_s. Labs with H800-specific traces should override via ac-auto-calibrate.
